text.title: Gemini Multimodal Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules for Gemini multimodal integrations.

rule.statement: Use Files API for any audio, video, or image total >4 MB. Inline base64 only for ≤4 MB total of images.
rule.rationale: Inline beyond 4 MB blows request size and is unreliable.

rule.statement: Poll file.state until ACTIVE before issuing generate_content; FAILED state requires re-upload.
rule.rationale: Files API is async; sending while PROCESSING returns errors.

rule.statement: Track 48h TTL on uploaded files; re-upload when nearing expiry. Do not assume files persist forever.
For documents reused ≥3 times AND ≥32K tokens, use context caching to cut input cost by ~75%.
rule.rationale: Cache amortises across calls; below threshold the cache-setup overhead exceeds savings.

rule.statement: Surface BLOCK_REASON identical to text calls; multimodal does NOT bypass safety.
rule.rationale: Block-on-image can otherwise become a silent empty response.

rule.statement: Set explicit mime_type when supplying inline parts (image/jpeg, image/png, audio/wav, video/mp4). Defaults are unreliable.
rule.rationale: Implicit mime inference fails on edge codecs.

rule.statement: Preconditions hold → wire Files API + polling + caching per the procedure.
